subject: DevOps
title: Container Services Without Kubernetes
description: A micro-lesson that makes Container Services Without Kubernetes usable.

hook.text: Kubernetes is powerful but complex. What if you just want to run one container, no cluster needed?

buildup.text: AWS Fargate runs containers without servers. Define your container, set CPU and memory, deploy. Done.

discovery.text: Azure Container Instances and Cloud Run offer the same idea. Push an image, get a running service.

twist.text: These services hide the cluster. No nodes, no scaling groups, no control planes to think about.

climax.text: Cloud Run scales to zero. No requests means no cost. Perfect for variable traffic or rare tools.

punchline.text: Not everything needs K8s. Simpler container services exist.

quiz.question: What does 'scale to zero' mean in services like Cloud Run?
options: No instances run when there's no traffic, so you pay nothing during idle time
The service deletes itself after each request
All data is erased when traffic stops
